Drone Multispectral Image Classification Pipeline

  • Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • View Source
Author

Thiago Orlando Costa Barboza

Published

March 16, 2026

1 Overview

This pipeline provides a complete workflow for multispectral drone image classification using machine learning models. Starting from a raw orthomosaic collected with a MicaSense camera, the pipeline covers:

  1. Radiometric normalization and raster clipping
  2. Interactive training sample digitizing
  3. ML model training (Random Forest, SVM, XGBoost)
  4. Model evaluation with confusion matrices and accuracy metrics
  5. Soil background removal
  6. Vegetation index extraction per treatment plot
Note

Interactive steps (marked with eval: false) require manual execution in the RStudio console. These include polygon digitizing with fieldView(editor = TRUE) and synchronized map comparisons. All other steps run automatically during rendering.

2 Installation

Code 
# Core packages
install.packages(c(
  "terra", "sf", "mapview", "stars", "caret", "mapedit",
  "dplyr", "fields", "leafem", "leafsync", "lwgeom",
  "BiocManager", "exactextractr", "xgboost", "nnet",
  "e1071", "randomForest", "kernlab", "RStoolbox",
  "ggplot2", "patchwork", "viridis", "scales", "writexl", "pliman"
))

# EBImage (required by FIELDimageR)
BiocManager::install("EBImage")

# FIELDimageR and FIELDimageR.Extra from GitHub
devtools::install_github("OpenDroneMap/FIELDimageR")
devtools::install_github("filipematias23/FIELDimageR.Extra")

3 Load Libraries

Code 
library(FIELDimageR)
library(FIELDimageR.Extra)
library(terra)
library(sf)
library(mapview)
library(leafsync)
library(RStoolbox)
library(caret)
library(randomForest)
library(kernlab)
library(e1071)
library(xgboost)
library(nnet)
library(pliman)
library(exactextractr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(viridis)
library(scales)
library(dplyr)
library(writexl)

4 Clip and Normalize the Orthomosaic

UAV imagery is typically collected with overlap beyond the field boundary. The first step is to clip the orthomosaic to the field polygon and ensure coordinate system compatibility between the raster and the polygon.

4.1 Why Divide MicaSense Imagery by 32,768?

When a MicaSense camera captures an image, the sensor does not directly measure reflectance — it measures electromagnetic energy converted into raw integer values known as Digital Numbers (DN). These raw values are influenced by solar irradiance, illumination angle, camera exposure, and sensor temperature, meaning they cannot be directly compared across different dates or flight conditions.

To address this, Metashape applies a radiometric calibration using a Calibrated Reflectance Panel (CRP) photographed before each flight, converting raw DNs into true surface reflectance. Metashape stores the output as 16-bit unsigned integers (UInt16) for storage efficiency, scaling reflectance values so that 32,768 (= 215, the midpoint of the UInt16 range) represents 100% reflectance. Dividing by 32,768 recovers the true reflectance scale (0.0 to ~1.2).

Values above 1.0 are physically valid — highly reflective surfaces such as white tarps or specular water bodies can exceed the panel reference. This applies to all MicaSense sensors: RedEdge, RedEdge-MX, RedEdge-MX Dual, Altum, Altum-PT, and RedEdge-P. The only exception is the thermal band (LWIR), stored in centi-Kelvin and converted as (value / 100) - 273.15 to obtain degrees Celsius.

4.2 Function Definition

Code 
prepare_raster_clip <- function(
    raster_path,               # Path to the raster file (.tif)
    polygon_path,              # Path to shapefile (.shp) or sf object
    target_crs       = NULL,   # Target EPSG code (e.g. 32723). NULL = use raster CRS
    micasense        = FALSE,  # TRUE if image was collected with a MicaSense camera
    auto_detect      = TRUE,   # Auto-detect high DN values and normalize
    micasense_factor = 32768,  # Normalization factor (MicaSense/Metashape default)
    force_normalize  = FALSE,  # Bypass safety check and force normalization
    output_path      = NULL,   # If provided, saves the clipped raster to disk
    verbose          = TRUE    # Print processing report
) {
  
  .log <- function(...) if (verbose) message(...)
  .sep <- function() if (verbose) message(strrep("-", 62))
  
  .sep()
  .log("  PREPARE_RASTER_CLIP -- Processing Report")
  .sep()
  .log("  [1/5] Loading input data...")
  
  r <- terra::rast(raster_path)
  
  shp <- if (inherits(polygon_path, "sf")) {
    polygon_path
  } else {
    sf::read_sf(polygon_path)
  }
  
  crs_raster_orig  <- terra::crs(r, describe = TRUE)
  crs_polygon_orig <- sf::st_crs(shp)
  res_orig         <- terra::res(r)
  nbands           <- terra::nlyr(r)
  band_names       <- names(r)
  dtype            <- terra::datatype(r)
  val_range        <- terra::minmax(r)
  global_min       <- min(val_range[1, ], na.rm = TRUE)
  global_max       <- max(val_range[2, ], na.rm = TRUE)
  epsg_raster_orig <- crs_raster_orig$code
  epsg_polygon_orig <- crs_polygon_orig$epsg
  
  .log("  [2/5] Checking coordinate reference systems...")
  
  epsg_target <- if (!is.null(target_crs)) target_crs else epsg_raster_orig
  crs_r_wkt   <- terra::crs(r)
  crs_shp_wkt <- sf::st_crs(shp)$wkt
  
  reproj_polygon <- FALSE
  if (!isTRUE(all.equal(crs_shp_wkt, crs_r_wkt))) {
    reproj_polygon <- TRUE
    shp <- sf::st_transform(shp, crs = epsg_target)
    .log("  [!] Polygon reprojected to EPSG:", epsg_target)
  } else {
    .log("  [OK] Polygon CRS is compatible with the raster.")
  }
  
  reproj_raster <- FALSE
  if (!is.null(target_crs) && epsg_raster_orig != as.character(target_crs)) {
    reproj_raster <- TRUE
    .log("  [!] Reprojecting raster to EPSG:", target_crs, "(this may take a while)...")
    r <- terra::project(r, paste0("EPSG:", target_crs))
    .log("  [OK] Raster successfully reprojected.")
  }
  
  epsg_raster_new <- terra::crs(r, describe = TRUE)$code
  res_new         <- terra::res(r)
  
  .log("  [3/5] Checking normalization requirements...")
  
  normalized         <- FALSE
  norm_reason        <- "None"
  already_normalized <- (global_max <= 2.0) && (global_min >= 0)
  
  if (already_normalized && !force_normalize) {
    .log("  [OK] Values already in reflectance scale (0.0 to ~1.0). No normalization applied.")
    if (micasense) {
      .log("  [WARNING] micasense = TRUE was set, but values appear already normalized.")
      .log("  [WARNING] Division by", micasense_factor, "was SKIPPED to prevent data corruption.")
      .log("  [WARNING] To force normalization anyway, set force_normalize = TRUE.")
    }
    norm_reason <- "Skipped -- values already in reflectance scale"
    
  } else if (micasense || force_normalize) {
    if (force_normalize && already_normalized)
      .log("  [!] force_normalize = TRUE. Overriding safety check.")
    .log("  [!] MicaSense camera declared. Applying division by", micasense_factor, "...")
    r           <- r / micasense_factor
    normalized  <- TRUE
    norm_reason <- paste0("MicaSense declared by user (/ ", micasense_factor, ")")
    
  } else if (auto_detect) {
    if (global_max > 1000 && global_max <= 65535) {
      detected_factor <- dplyr::case_when(
        global_max > 30000 & global_max < 36000  ~ 32768,
        global_max > 32767 & global_max <= 65535 ~ 65535,
        global_max > 1000  & global_max <= 10000 ~ 10000,
        TRUE                                     ~ micasense_factor
      )
      .log(sprintf("  [!] High DN values detected (max = %.1f). Applying division by %d...",
                   global_max, detected_factor))
      r           <- r / detected_factor
      normalized  <- TRUE
      norm_reason <- sprintf("Auto-detected (max = %.1f, / %d)", global_max, detected_factor)
    } else {
      .log("  [OK] No normalization needed based on auto-detection.")
      norm_reason <- "Skipped -- no high DN values detected"
    }
  } else {
    .log("  [OK] Normalization skipped (micasense = FALSE, auto_detect = FALSE).")
    norm_reason <- "Skipped -- normalization disabled by user"
  }
  
  .log("  [4/5] Clipping raster with polygon boundary...")
  shp_vect  <- terra::vect(shp)
  r_clipped <- terra::mask(terra::crop(r, shp_vect), shp_vect)
  
  if (!is.null(output_path)) {
    .log("  [5/5] Saving output raster to:", output_path)
    terra::writeRaster(r_clipped, output_path, overwrite = TRUE, datatype = "FLT4S")
  } else {
    .log("  [5/5] Output returned as in-memory SpatRaster (output_path not provided).")
  }
  
  new_range   <- terra::minmax(r_clipped)
  clipped_min <- min(new_range[1, ], na.rm = TRUE)
  clipped_max <- max(new_range[2, ], na.rm = TRUE)
  
  .sep()
  .log("  PROCESSING REPORT")
  .sep()
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %s", "Input raster:",   basename(raster_path)))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %s", "Input polygon:",  basename(if (is.character(polygon_path)) polygon_path else "sf object")))
  .log("")
  .log("  -- Pixel Resolution ----------------------------------------")
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %.4f x %.4f m", "Original resolution (X x Y):", res_orig[1], res_orig[2]))
  if (reproj_raster)
    .log(sprintf("  %-32s %.4f x %.4f m", "Resolution after reprojection:", res_new[1], res_new[2]))
  .log("")
  .log("  -- Coordinate Reference Systems ----------------------------")
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s EPSG:%-6s (%s)", "Raster CRS (original):",
               epsg_raster_orig, terra::crs(terra::rast(raster_path), describe = TRUE)$name))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s EPSG:%-6s", "Raster CRS (final):", epsg_raster_new))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s EPSG:%-6s (%s)", "Polygon CRS (original):",
               epsg_polygon_orig, crs_polygon_orig$Name))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s EPSG:%-6s", "Polygon CRS (final):", sf::st_crs(shp)$epsg))
  .log("")
  .log("  -- Bands & Pixel Values ------------------------------------")
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %d  (%s)", "Number of bands:", nbands, paste(band_names, collapse = ", ")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %s", "Data type:", paste(unique(dtype), collapse = ", ")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %.4f", "Original minimum value:", global_min))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %.4f", "Original maximum value:", global_max))
  .log("")
  .log("  -- Normalization -------------------------------------------")
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %s", "MicaSense camera:",
               ifelse(micasense, "YES (declared by user)", "NOT declared")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %s", "Already normalized (detected):",
               ifelse(already_normalized, "YES", "NO")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %s", "Force normalize:", ifelse(force_normalize, "YES", "NO")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %s", "Normalization applied:", ifelse(normalized, "YES", "NO")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %s", "Reason:", norm_reason))
  .log("")
  .log("  -- Clipped Output ------------------------------------------")
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %.4f  to  %.4f", "Value range (clipped):", clipped_min, clipped_max))
  if (normalized && clipped_max > 1.0) {
    .log("  NOTE: Values above 1.0 are valid -- highly reflective surfaces")
    .log("  (white tarps, specular water) can exceed the panel reference.")
  }
  .sep()
  .log("  [DONE] Processing complete.\n")
  
  return(invisible(r_clipped))
}

Usage

Important

Run in the console. This step reads large raster files from disk.

Code 
result <- prepare_raster_clip(
  raster_path      = "C:/Users/Thiago/Desktop/soil_classification/mica/orto_06_21_24.tif",
  polygon_path     = "C:/Users/Thiago/Desktop/soil_classification/contour_IH_geojson.geojson",
  target_crs       = 32617,
  micasense        = TRUE,
  auto_detect      = TRUE,
  micasense_factor = 32768,
  force_normalize  = FALSE,
  output_path      = "C:/Users/Thiago/Desktop/soil_classification/mica/06_21_24_clipped.tif",
  verbose          = TRUE
)

# Visualize clipped result
polygon <- read_sf("C:/Users/Thiago/Desktop/soil_classification/contour_IH_geojson.geojson")
fieldView(result, polygon)
[1] "Starting analysis ..."
[1] "End!"

5 Create Training Samples

After clipping the image, random training samples must be digitized manually. Polygons are drawn interactively over representative areas of each class, and random points are generated inside each polygon.

Important considerations:

  • Shadows present distinct reflectance values from soil. They can be treated as a separate class or merged with the soil class depending on the study objectives.
  • Polygon size matters: small polygons may not capture the full spectral variability of a class. For soils with visually distinct colors (sand vs. clay), samples should cover all variations.
  • The display mode can be set to "NDVI", "GNDVI", or "NDRE" instead of "RGB" to enhance contrast between soil and plants before digitizing.

5.1 Function Definition

Code 
generate_training_samples <- function(
    mosaic,
    class_names,
    n_samples    = 400,
    display      = "RGB",
    bands        = list(red = 1, green = 2, blue = 3,
                        nir = NULL, rededge = NULL, swir = NULL),
    output_path  = NULL,
    verbose      = TRUE,
    dsample      = 5
) {
  
  .log <- function(...) if (verbose) message(...)
  .sep <- function() if (verbose) message(strrep("-", 62))
  
  if (!is.character(class_names) || length(class_names) < 2)
    stop("  [ERROR] 'class_names' must be a character vector with at least 2 classes.")
  
  display <- toupper(display)
  valid_displays <- c("RGB", "NDVI", "GNDVI", "NDRE")
  if (!display %in% valid_displays)
    stop(sprintf("  [ERROR] 'display' must be one of: %s",
                 paste(valid_displays, collapse = ", ")))
  
  if (display %in% c("NDVI", "GNDVI", "NDRE") && is.null(bands$nir))
    stop(sprintf("  [ERROR] display = '%s' requires bands$nir to be set.", display))
  if (display == "NDRE" && is.null(bands$rededge))
    stop("  [ERROR] display = 'NDRE' requires bands$rededge to be set.")
  if (display == "RGB" && (is.null(bands$red) || is.null(bands$green) || is.null(bands$blue)))
    stop("  [ERROR] display = 'RGB' requires bands$red, bands$green, and bands$blue.")
  
  if (length(n_samples) == 1) {
    n_samples <- setNames(rep(n_samples, length(class_names)), class_names)
  } else {
    if (!all(class_names %in% names(n_samples)))
      stop("  [ERROR] When 'n_samples' is a vector, all class names must be named.")
  }
  
  n_classes <- length(class_names)
  .get_band <- function(idx) terra::subset(mosaic, idx)
  
  display_mosaic <- switch(display,
    "RGB"   = terra::subset(mosaic, c(bands$red, bands$green, bands$blue)),
    "NDVI"  = { idx <- (.get_band(bands$nir) - .get_band(bands$red))   / (.get_band(bands$nir) + .get_band(bands$red));   names(idx) <- "NDVI";  idx },
    "GNDVI" = { idx <- (.get_band(bands$nir) - .get_band(bands$green)) / (.get_band(bands$nir) + .get_band(bands$green)); names(idx) <- "GNDVI"; idx },
    "NDRE"  = { idx <- (.get_band(bands$nir) - .get_band(bands$rededge)) / (.get_band(bands$nir) + .get_band(bands$rededge)); names(idx) <- "NDRE"; idx }
  )
  
  .sep()
  .log("  GENERATE_TRAINING_SAMPLES")
  .sep()
  .log(sprintf("  %-22s %d", "Number of classes:", n_classes))
  .log(sprintf("  %-22s %s", "Classes:", paste(class_names, collapse = ", ")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-22s %s", "Samples per class:",
               paste(paste0(class_names, "=", n_samples[class_names]), collapse = ", ")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-22s %s", "Display mode:", display))
  .sep()
  
  sample_list <- vector("list", length = n_classes)
  
  for (i in seq_along(class_names)) {
    cls  <- class_names[i]
    npts <- n_samples[[cls]]
    .log(sprintf("\n  [%d/%d] Class: '%s'  |  Target samples: %d  |  Display: %s",
                 i, n_classes, cls, npts, display))
    .log("  >> Draw one or more polygons in the editor window, then close it.")
    
    polygons <- fieldView(mosaic = display_mosaic, editor = TRUE, downsample = dsample)
    
    if (is.null(polygons) || nrow(polygons) == 0)
      stop(sprintf("  [ERROR] No polygons digitized for class '%s'. Aborting.", cls))
    
    pts       <- sf::st_as_sf(sf::st_sample(polygons, npts))
    pts$class <- cls
    sample_list[[i]] <- pts
    .log(sprintf("  [OK] %d points generated for class '%s'.", nrow(pts), cls))
  }
  
  .sep()
  .log("  Combining all classes into a single training dataset...")
  training_samples       <- do.call(rbind, sample_list)
  training_samples$class <- factor(training_samples$class, levels = class_names)
  
  if (!is.null(output_path)) {
    sf::st_write(training_samples, output_path, delete_dsn = TRUE, quiet = TRUE)
    .log(sprintf("  [OK] File saved to: %s", output_path))
  }
  
  .sep()
  .log("  SUMMARY")
  .sep()
  .log(sprintf("  %-22s %d", "Total samples:", nrow(training_samples)))
  for (cls in class_names) {
    n   <- sum(training_samples$class == cls)
    pct <- round(n / nrow(training_samples) * 100, 1)
    .log(sprintf("  %-22s %d  (%.1f%%)", paste0("  ", cls, ":"), n, pct))
  }
  .sep()
  .log("  [DONE] Training samples ready.\n")
  
  return(invisible(training_samples))
}

Usage

Important

Interactive step — run in the console only.
fieldView(editor = TRUE) opens an interactive window that cannot render during HTML/PDF generation.

Code 
options(warn = -1)
# Step 1: digitize polygons interactively and save to disk
training_samples <- generate_training_samples(
  mosaic      = result,
  class_names = c("soil", "plants"),
  n_samples   = 400,
  display     = "GNDVI",
  bands       = list(red = 3, green = 2, blue = 1, nir = 5, rededge = 4),
  output_path = NULL,
  dsample     = 2
)
options(warn = 0)
# Visualize samples over the original raster
fieldView(result, training_samples)
Code 
# Step 2: load pre-saved samples 
training_samples <- sf::read_sf("C:/Users/Thiago/Desktop/soil_classification/training_samples.gpkg")
glimpse(training_samples)

Columns: 2
$ class <chr> "soil", "soil", "soil", "soil", "soil", "soil", "soil", "soil", …

Code 
print(training_samples)

# ℹ 790 more rows

6 Machine Learning Classification

Three machine learning models are trained and compared:

Model Algorithm Package
rf Random Forest randomForest
svmRadial Support Vector Machine (RBF kernel) kernlab
xgbTree Extreme Gradient Boosting xgboost

All models use k-fold cross-validation and hyperparameter tuning via caret::train(). The train_partition parameter controls the train/test split (default: 70% training, 30% test).

6.1 Function Definition

Code 
run_classification_models <- function(
    raster_data,
    training_data,
    models          = c("rf", "svmRadial", "xgbTree"),
    response_col    = "class",
    tune_length     = 3,
    kfold           = 5,
    train_partition = 0.7,
    min_dist        = 0,
    plot_results    = TRUE,
    output_dir      = NULL,
    verbose         = TRUE
) {
  
  .log <- function(...) if (verbose) message(...)
  .sep <- function() if (verbose) message(strrep("-", 62))
  
  .sep()
  .log("  RUN_CLASSIFICATION_MODELS -- Processing Report")
  .sep()
  
  if (!response_col %in% names(training_data))
    stop(sprintf("  [ERROR] Column '%s' not found in training_data.", response_col))
  if (length(models) == 0)
    stop("  [ERROR] 'models' must contain at least one model name.")
  if (train_partition <= 0 || train_partition >= 1)
    stop("  [ERROR] 'train_partition' must be between 0 and 1 (e.g. 0.7).")
  
  if (!is.null(output_dir) && !dir.exists(output_dir)) {
    dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    .log(sprintf("  [!] Output directory created: %s", output_dir))
  }
  
  names(raster_data) <- make.unique(names(raster_data))
  if (inherits(training_data, "sf"))
    training_data <- as(training_data, "Spatial")
  
  n_classes  <- length(unique(training_data[[response_col]]))
  class_lvls <- sort(unique(training_data[[response_col]]))
  n_total    <- nrow(training_data)
  n_train    <- round(n_total * train_partition)
  n_test     <- n_total - n_train
  n_bands    <- terra::nlyr(raster_data)
  
  .log(sprintf("  %-26s %s",       "Models to train:",   paste(models, collapse = ", ")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-26s %d  (%s)", "Classes:",           n_classes, paste(class_lvls, collapse = ", ")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-26s %d  (train: %d | test: %d)", "Total samples:", n_total, n_train, n_test))
  .log(sprintf("  %-26s %d",       "Raster bands:",      n_bands))
  .log(sprintf("  %-26s %d-fold",  "Cross-validation:",  kfold))
  .log(sprintf("  %-26s %d",       "Tune length:",       tune_length))
  .sep()
  
  results       <- list()
  metrics_list  <- list()
  failed_models <- character(0)
  
  for (i in seq_along(models)) {
    mdl <- models[i]
    .log(sprintf("\n  [%d/%d] Training model: %s ...", i, length(models), mdl))
    
    model_result <- tryCatch({
      suppressWarnings(
      RStoolbox::superClass(
        raster_data,
        trainData      = training_data,
        responseCol    = response_col,
        model          = mdl,
        tuneLength     = tune_length,
        kfold          = kfold,
        trainPartition = train_partition,
        minDist        = min_dist
       )
      )
    }, error = function(e) {
      .log(sprintf("  [ERROR] Model '%s' failed: %s", mdl, conditionMessage(e)))
      NULL
    })
    
    if (is.null(model_result)) { failed_models <- c(failed_models, mdl); next }
    .log(sprintf("  [OK] Model '%s' trained successfully.", mdl))
    
    perf         <- model_result$validation$performance
    perf_overall <- perf$overall
    perf_class   <- perf$byClass
    
    required <- c("Accuracy", "Kappa", "AccuracyLower", "AccuracyUpper")
    missing  <- setdiff(required, names(perf_overall))
    if (length(missing) > 0)
      .log(sprintf("  [WARNING] Missing metrics in $overall for '%s': %s",
                   mdl, paste(missing, collapse = ", ")))
    
    metrics_list[[mdl]] <- data.frame(
      model        = mdl,
      overall_acc  = as.numeric(perf_overall["Accuracy"]),
      kappa        = as.numeric(perf_overall["Kappa"]),
      acc_lower    = as.numeric(perf_overall["AccuracyLower"]),
      acc_upper    = as.numeric(perf_overall["AccuracyUpper"]),
      sensitivity  = as.numeric(perf_class["Sensitivity"]),
      specificity  = as.numeric(perf_class["Specificity"]),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    
    if (plot_results) {
      plot(model_result$map, main = sprintf("Classified Map -- %s", toupper(mdl)))
      print(perf$table)
      print(round(perf_overall, 4))
    }
    
    if (!is.null(output_dir)) {
      out_file <- file.path(output_dir, sprintf("classified_map_%s.tif", mdl))
      terra::writeRaster(model_result$map, out_file, overwrite = TRUE)
      .log(sprintf("  [OK] Map saved: %s", out_file))
    }
    
    results[[mdl]] <- model_result
  }
  
  .sep()
  .log("  ACCURACY COMPARISON")
  .sep()
  
  if (length(metrics_list) > 0) {
    metrics_df           <- do.call(rbind, metrics_list)
    metrics_df           <- metrics_df[order(-metrics_df$overall_acc), ]
    rownames(metrics_df) <- NULL
    
    .log(sprintf("  %-14s  %-10s  %-8s  %-10s  %-10s  %-20s",
                 "Model", "Accuracy", "Kappa", "Sensitiv.", "Specific.", "95% CI"))
    .log(sprintf("  %s", strrep(".", 76)))
    for (j in seq_len(nrow(metrics_df))) {
      row <- metrics_df[j, ]
      .log(sprintf("  %-14s  %-10s  %-8s  %-10s  %-10s  [%.4f -- %.4f]",
                   row$model, sprintf("%.4f", row$overall_acc),
                   sprintf("%.4f", row$kappa),
                   sprintf("%.4f", row$sensitivity),
                   sprintf("%.4f", row$specificity),
                   row$acc_lower, row$acc_upper))
    }
    best_model <- metrics_df$model[1]
    .log(sprintf("\n  Best model (overall accuracy): %s  (%.4f)",
                 best_model, metrics_df$overall_acc[1]))
    results[["metrics"]] <- metrics_df
  } else {
    .log("  [WARNING] No models completed successfully.")
  }
  
  if (length(failed_models) > 0) {
    .sep()
    .log("  [WARNING] The following models failed and were skipped:")
    for (fm in failed_models) .log(sprintf("            -- %s", fm))
  }
  
  n_completed <- length(results) - as.integer("metrics" %in% names(results))
  .sep()
  .log(sprintf("  [DONE] %d/%d models completed successfully.\n", n_completed, length(models)))
  
  return(invisible(results))
}

Usage

Code 
options(xgboost.verbosity = 0)

band_result <- run_classification_models(
  raster_data     = result,
  training_data   = training_samples,
  models          = c("rf", "svmRadial", "xgbTree"),
  kfold           = 5,
  train_partition = 0.7,
  plot_results    = FALSE,
  verbose = TRUE
)

Code 
# Accuracy comparison table
band_result$metrics
      model overall_acc    kappa acc_lower acc_upper sensitivity specificity
1        rf   1.0000000 1.000000 0.9743565 1.0000000           1   1.0000000
2   xgbTree   0.9929078 0.984575 0.9611195 0.9998205           1   0.9890110
3 svmRadial   0.9785714 0.954955 0.9386550 0.9955589           1   0.9655172
Code 
options(xgboost.verbosity = NULL)

7 Model Evaluation

Classification performance is evaluated using the following metrics, computed per class via one-vs-rest decomposition from the confusion matrix:

Metric Formula Description
Accuracy (TP + TN) / total Overall correct classifications
Kappa (Acc − E) / (1 − E) Chance-corrected agreement
Sensitivity TP / (TP + FN) Producer’s accuracy / Recall
Specificity TN / (TN + FP) True negative rate
Precision TP / (TP + FP) User’s accuracy
F1 Score 2 × (P × R) / (P + R) Harmonic mean of Precision and Recall
Balanced Accuracy (Sens + Spec) / 2 Robust to class imbalance

7.1 Function Definition

Code 
plot_classification_results <- function(
    band_result,
    save_dir,
    dpi     = 300,
    verbose = TRUE
) {
  
  .log <- function(...) if (verbose) message(...)
  .sep <- function() if (verbose) message(strrep("-", 62))
  
  .sep()
  .log("  PLOT_CLASSIFICATION_RESULTS -- Processing Report")
  .sep()
  
  if (!dir.exists(save_dir)) {
    dir.create(save_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    .log(sprintf("  [!] Output directory created: %s", save_dir))
  }
  
  model_keys <- setdiff(names(band_result), "metrics")
  if (length(model_keys) == 0)
    stop("  [ERROR] No model results found in band_result.")
  
  .log(sprintf("  %-24s %s", "Models found:", paste(model_keys, collapse = ", ")))
  .sep()
  .log("  [1/4] Extracting metrics from confusion matrices...")
  
  overall_rows <- list()
  byclass_rows <- list()
  cm_list      <- list()
  
  for (mdl in model_keys) {
    perf <- band_result[[mdl]]$validation$performance
    ov   <- perf$overall
    tbl  <- perf$table
    
    overall_rows[[mdl]] <- data.frame(
      model    = toupper(mdl),
      metric   = c("Accuracy", "Kappa"),
      value    = as.numeric(c(ov["Accuracy"], ov["Kappa"])),
      ci_lower = as.numeric(c(ov["AccuracyLower"], NA)),
      ci_upper = as.numeric(c(ov["AccuracyUpper"], NA)),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    
    classes <- rownames(tbl)
    for (cls in classes) {
      tp      <- tbl[cls, cls]
      fn      <- sum(tbl[cls, ]) - tp
      fp      <- sum(tbl[, cls]) - tp
      tn      <- sum(tbl) - tp - fn - fp
      sens    <- tp / (tp + fn)
      spec    <- tn / (tn + fp)
      prec    <- ifelse((tp + fp) > 0, tp / (tp + fp), NA)
      rec     <- sens
      f1      <- ifelse(!is.na(prec) & (prec + rec) > 0, 2 * prec * rec / (prec + rec), NA)
      bal_acc <- (sens + spec) / 2
      
      met_vals <- c(Sensitivity = sens, Specificity = spec, Precision = prec,
                    F1 = f1, `Balanced Accuracy` = bal_acc)
      
      for (met in names(met_vals)) {
        byclass_rows[[paste(mdl, cls, met)]] <- data.frame(
          model = toupper(mdl), class = cls, metric = met,
          value = as.numeric(met_vals[[met]]), stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      }
    }
    cm_list[[mdl]] <- tbl
    .log(sprintf("  [OK] '%s' -- classes: %s", toupper(mdl), paste(classes, collapse = ", ")))
  }
  
  overall_df           <- do.call(rbind, overall_rows)
  byclass_df           <- do.call(rbind, byclass_rows)
  rownames(overall_df) <- NULL
  rownames(byclass_df) <- NULL
  
  base_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 13),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 11), axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
          legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
          strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
  
  model_colors <- setNames(viridis::viridis(length(model_keys), option = "D", end = 0.85),
                           toupper(model_keys))
  class_colors <- setNames(viridis::viridis(length(unique(byclass_df$class)), option = "turbo", end = 0.85),
                           unique(byclass_df$class))
  
  .log("  [2/4] Plotting confusion matrices...")
  cm_plots <- list()
  for (mdl in model_keys) {
    cm_df         <- as.data.frame(as.table(cm_list[[mdl]]))
    colnames(cm_df) <- c("Predicted", "Reference", "Count")
    totals        <- aggregate(Count ~ Reference, data = cm_df, FUN = sum)
    cm_df         <- merge(cm_df, totals, by = "Reference", suffixes = c("", "_total"))
    cm_df$pct     <- cm_df$Count / cm_df$Count_total * 100
    cm_plots[[mdl]] <- ggplot(cm_df, aes(x = Reference, y = Predicted, fill = Count)) +
      geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
      geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%d\n(%.1f%%)", Count, pct)),
                size = 4, fontface = "bold", color = "white") +
      scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "D", direction = -1, name = "Count") +
      labs(title = sprintf("Confusion Matrix -- %s", toupper(mdl)),
           x = "True Class", y = "Predicted Class") +
      coord_equal() + base_theme + theme(legend.position = "right")
  }
  combined_cm <- wrap_plots(cm_plots, nrow = 1)
  ggsave(file.path(save_dir, "confusion_matrices.png"), combined_cm,
         width = 5.5 * length(model_keys), height = 5.5, dpi = dpi)
  .log("  [OK] Confusion matrices saved.")
  
  .log("  [3/4] Plotting overall metrics...")
  overall_plot <- ggplot(overall_df, aes(x = model, y = value, fill = model)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.65, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_errorbar(data = overall_df[!is.na(overall_df$ci_lower), ],
                  aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper), width = 0.18, linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.4f", value)), vjust = -0.55, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = model_colors) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.12), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
                       labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
    facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y") +
    labs(title = "Overall Model Performance", x = NULL, y = "Value") +
    base_theme + theme(legend.position = "none")
  ggsave(file.path(save_dir, "overall_metrics.png"), overall_plot,
         width = 4 * length(unique(overall_df$metric)), height = 5, dpi = dpi)
  .log("  [OK] Overall metrics plot saved.")
  
  .log("  [4/4] Plotting per-class metrics...")
  byclass_clean        <- byclass_df[!is.na(byclass_df$value), ]
  byclass_clean$metric <- factor(byclass_clean$metric,
                                 levels = c("Sensitivity", "Specificity", "Precision",
                                            "F1", "Balanced Accuracy"))
  byclass_plot <- ggplot(byclass_clean, aes(x = model, y = value, fill = class)) +
    geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), width = 0.7,
             color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", value)), position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
              vjust = -0.55, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = class_colors, name = "Class") +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.12), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
                       labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
    facet_wrap(~ metric, nrow = 2) +
    labs(title = "Per-Class Model Performance", x = "Model", y = "Value") +
    base_theme + theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  ggsave(file.path(save_dir, "byclass_metrics.png"), byclass_plot,
         width = max(10, 3.5 * ceiling(length(unique(byclass_clean$metric)) / 2)),
         height = 10, dpi = dpi)
  .log("  [OK] Per-class metrics plot saved.")
  
  .sep()
  .log("  SUMMARY")
  .sep()
  .log(sprintf("  %-30s %s", "Models evaluated:", paste(toupper(model_keys), collapse = ", ")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-30s %s", "Classes found:", paste(unique(byclass_df$class), collapse = ", ")))
  acc_rows <- overall_df[overall_df$metric == "Accuracy", ]
  acc_rows <- acc_rows[order(-acc_rows$value), ]
  .log("")
  .log("  -- Overall Accuracy ------------------------------------------")
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(acc_rows)))
    .log(sprintf("  %-14s %.4f  [%.4f -- %.4f]",
                 acc_rows$model[i], acc_rows$value[i],
                 acc_rows$ci_lower[i], acc_rows$ci_upper[i]))
  .sep()
  .log(sprintf("  [DONE] All plots saved to: %s\n", save_dir))
  
  return(invisible(list(
    overall_df = overall_df, byclass_df = byclass_df,
    overall_plot = overall_plot, byclass_plot = byclass_plot,
    confusion_matrix = combined_cm
  )))
}

Usage

Code 
metrics_output <- plot_classification_results(
  band_result = band_result,
  save_dir    = "C:/Users/Thiago/Desktop/soil_classification/mica/save_test/TEST",
  dpi         = 600
)

metrics_output$overall_plot
Code 
metrics_output$byclass_plot
Code 
metrics_output$confusion_matrix

8 Soil Background Removal

Using the best classification model, soil pixels are masked out, retaining only plant pixels for subsequent vegetation index analysis. This step reduces noise in spectral index calculations caused by soil background.

Code 
# Create mask: keep only pixels classified as plants (class == 1)
soilmask <- band_result$rf$map == 1
ortosolo <- mask(result, soilmask, maskvalue = 0)

# Save masked raster
#writeRaster(ortosolo, "results/06_21_removed.tif", overwrite = TRUE)

# Compare original vs masked using synchronized maps
m1 <- mapview(raster::raster(result[[1]]),
              layer.name  = "Original",
              col.regions = gray.colors(100))
              #maxpixels   = 7009735)

m2 <- mapview(raster::raster(ortosolo),
              layer.name = "Soil Removed")
              #maxpixels  = 7009735)

sync(m1, m2)

9 Treatment Plots and Buffer

Treatment plots are loaded from a geospatial file and a negative buffer is applied to avoid edge effects between adjacent plots. The buffer reduces pixel contamination at plot boundaries.

Code 
plots_buffer <- function(file,
                         based        = ortosolo,
                         crs          = 32617,
                         buffer_value = -1,
                         view         = TRUE) {
  
  plt <- st_read(file)
  plt <- st_transform(x = plt, crs = crs)
  plt <- st_buffer(x = plt, dist = buffer_value)
  
  if (view == TRUE) {
    print(fieldView(based, fieldShape = plt, type = 2, alpha_grid = 0.2))
  } else {
    print(plot(plt))
  }
  
  return(invisible(plt))
}
Code 
trts <- plots_buffer(
  file         = "C:/Users/Thiago/Desktop/soil_classification/IH_plots_USDA_NxB_geojson.geojson",
  based        = ortosolo,
  crs          = 32617,
  buffer_value = -0.2,
  view         = TRUE
)
Reading layer `IH_plots_USDA_NxB_geojson' from data source 
  `C:\Users\Thiago\Desktop\soil_classification\IH_plots_USDA_NxB_geojson.geojson' 
  using driver `GeoJSON'
Simple feature collection with 72 features and 6 fields
Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON
Dimension:     XY
Bounding box:  xmin: 285998.8 ymin: 3732910 xmax: 286109.6 ymax: 3733190
Projected CRS: WGS 84 / UTM zone 17N
[1] "Starting analysis ..."
[1] "End!"

10 Vegetation Index Calculation

Vegetation indices are calculated using the pliman package, which provides over 30 pre-configured indices. Three alternative packages are available depending on the application:

Package Indices Notes
pliman 30+ Fastest for large rasters
RStoolbox 60+ Most comprehensive
FIELDimageR ~20 Integrated with fieldView
Tip

Memory note: Vegetation index calculations are RAM-intensive. On systems with 8 GB RAM, limit the number of indices to a maximum of 10 simultaneously.

Code 
vi_calculation <- mosaic_index(
  mosaic = ortosolo,
  r      = 3,
  g      = 2,
  b      = 1,
  re     = 4,
  nir    = 5,
  index  = c("R", "G", "NDVI", "NDRE", "GNDVI",
             "NGRDI", "RVI", "SAVI", "TSAVI", "TVI",
             "CVI", "CIG", "BAI", "OSAVI", "MSR",
             "NLI", "GOSAVI", "RDVI", "TDVI", "ARI",
             "MSAVI", "VIG", "WDRVI")
)
Code 
# Visualize one VI
fieldView(mosaic = vi_calculation$NDVI, fieldShape = trts, plotCol = "Ntreat")
[1] "Starting analysis ..."
[1] "End!"

11 Extract Statistics per Plot

Vegetation index values are extracted for each treatment plot and summarized using the median (or any other statistic). The output is a data frame with one row per plot, ready for statistical analysis.

Code 
stats <- cbind(
  st_drop_geometry(trts),
  exact_extract(vi_calculation, trts, fun = "median")
) %>%
  rename_with(~ gsub("median\\.", "", .x))

# Save to Excel
#writexl::write_xlsx(stats, "results/vegetation_indices.xlsx")

# Preview
head(stats)

Source Code
---
title: "Drone Multispectral Image Classification Pipeline"
author: "Thiago Orlando Costa Barboza"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    code-copy: true
    theme: flatly
    highlight-style: github
    self-contained: false
    fig-align: center
    number-sections: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
  eval: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| echo: false
#| eval: true

# Packages loaded silently for document rendering
# (functions are defined but not executed during render)
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(ggplot2)
})
```

## Overview

This pipeline provides a complete workflow for **multispectral drone image 
classification** using machine learning models. Starting from a raw orthomosaic 
collected with a MicaSense camera, the pipeline covers:

1. Radiometric normalization and raster clipping
2. Interactive training sample digitizing
3. ML model training (Random Forest, SVM, XGBoost)
4. Model evaluation with confusion matrices and accuracy metrics
5. Soil background removal
6. Vegetation index extraction per treatment plot

::: {.callout-note}
**Interactive steps** (marked with `eval: false`) require manual execution in 
the RStudio console. These include polygon digitizing with `fieldView(editor = TRUE)` 
and synchronized map comparisons. All other steps run automatically during rendering.
:::

---

## Installation

```{r}
#| label: install
#| eval: false

# Core packages
install.packages(c(
  "terra", "sf", "mapview", "stars", "caret", "mapedit",
  "dplyr", "fields", "leafem", "leafsync", "lwgeom",
  "BiocManager", "exactextractr", "xgboost", "nnet",
  "e1071", "randomForest", "kernlab", "RStoolbox",
  "ggplot2", "patchwork", "viridis", "scales", "writexl", "pliman"
))

# EBImage (required by FIELDimageR)
BiocManager::install("EBImage")

# FIELDimageR and FIELDimageR.Extra from GitHub
devtools::install_github("OpenDroneMap/FIELDimageR")
devtools::install_github("filipematias23/FIELDimageR.Extra")
```

---

## Load Libraries

```{r}
#| label: libraries
#| eval: true

library(FIELDimageR)
library(FIELDimageR.Extra)
library(terra)
library(sf)
library(mapview)
library(leafsync)
library(RStoolbox)
library(caret)
library(randomForest)
library(kernlab)
library(e1071)
library(xgboost)
library(nnet)
library(pliman)
library(exactextractr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(viridis)
library(scales)
library(dplyr)
library(writexl)
```

---

## Clip and Normalize the Orthomosaic {#sec-clip}

UAV imagery is typically collected with overlap beyond the field boundary.
The first step is to clip the orthomosaic to the field polygon and ensure
coordinate system compatibility between the raster and the polygon.

### Why Divide MicaSense Imagery by 32,768?

When a MicaSense camera captures an image, the sensor does not directly measure 
reflectance — it measures electromagnetic energy converted into raw integer values 
known as Digital Numbers (DN). These raw values are influenced by solar irradiance, 
illumination angle, camera exposure, and sensor temperature, meaning they cannot be 
directly compared across different dates or flight conditions.

To address this, Metashape applies a radiometric calibration using a **Calibrated 
Reflectance Panel (CRP)** photographed before each flight, converting raw DNs into 
true surface reflectance. Metashape stores the output as **16-bit unsigned integers 
(UInt16)** for storage efficiency, scaling reflectance values so that **32,768 
(= 2^15^, the midpoint of the UInt16 range) represents 100% reflectance**. Dividing 
by 32,768 recovers the true reflectance scale (0.0 to ~1.2).

Values **above 1.0 are physically valid** — highly reflective surfaces such as white 
tarps or specular water bodies can exceed the panel reference. This applies to all 
MicaSense sensors: RedEdge, RedEdge-MX, RedEdge-MX Dual, Altum, Altum-PT, and 
RedEdge-P. The only exception is the thermal band (LWIR), stored in centi-Kelvin 
and converted as `(value / 100) - 273.15` to obtain degrees Celsius.

### Function Definition

```{r}
#| label: fn-prepare-raster
#| eval: true

prepare_raster_clip <- function(
    raster_path,               # Path to the raster file (.tif)
    polygon_path,              # Path to shapefile (.shp) or sf object
    target_crs       = NULL,   # Target EPSG code (e.g. 32723). NULL = use raster CRS
    micasense        = FALSE,  # TRUE if image was collected with a MicaSense camera
    auto_detect      = TRUE,   # Auto-detect high DN values and normalize
    micasense_factor = 32768,  # Normalization factor (MicaSense/Metashape default)
    force_normalize  = FALSE,  # Bypass safety check and force normalization
    output_path      = NULL,   # If provided, saves the clipped raster to disk
    verbose          = TRUE    # Print processing report
) {
  
  .log <- function(...) if (verbose) message(...)
  .sep <- function() if (verbose) message(strrep("-", 62))
  
  .sep()
  .log("  PREPARE_RASTER_CLIP -- Processing Report")
  .sep()
  .log("  [1/5] Loading input data...")
  
  r <- terra::rast(raster_path)
  
  shp <- if (inherits(polygon_path, "sf")) {
    polygon_path
  } else {
    sf::read_sf(polygon_path)
  }
  
  crs_raster_orig  <- terra::crs(r, describe = TRUE)
  crs_polygon_orig <- sf::st_crs(shp)
  res_orig         <- terra::res(r)
  nbands           <- terra::nlyr(r)
  band_names       <- names(r)
  dtype            <- terra::datatype(r)
  val_range        <- terra::minmax(r)
  global_min       <- min(val_range[1, ], na.rm = TRUE)
  global_max       <- max(val_range[2, ], na.rm = TRUE)
  epsg_raster_orig <- crs_raster_orig$code
  epsg_polygon_orig <- crs_polygon_orig$epsg
  
  .log("  [2/5] Checking coordinate reference systems...")
  
  epsg_target <- if (!is.null(target_crs)) target_crs else epsg_raster_orig
  crs_r_wkt   <- terra::crs(r)
  crs_shp_wkt <- sf::st_crs(shp)$wkt
  
  reproj_polygon <- FALSE
  if (!isTRUE(all.equal(crs_shp_wkt, crs_r_wkt))) {
    reproj_polygon <- TRUE
    shp <- sf::st_transform(shp, crs = epsg_target)
    .log("  [!] Polygon reprojected to EPSG:", epsg_target)
  } else {
    .log("  [OK] Polygon CRS is compatible with the raster.")
  }
  
  reproj_raster <- FALSE
  if (!is.null(target_crs) && epsg_raster_orig != as.character(target_crs)) {
    reproj_raster <- TRUE
    .log("  [!] Reprojecting raster to EPSG:", target_crs, "(this may take a while)...")
    r <- terra::project(r, paste0("EPSG:", target_crs))
    .log("  [OK] Raster successfully reprojected.")
  }
  
  epsg_raster_new <- terra::crs(r, describe = TRUE)$code
  res_new         <- terra::res(r)
  
  .log("  [3/5] Checking normalization requirements...")
  
  normalized         <- FALSE
  norm_reason        <- "None"
  already_normalized <- (global_max <= 2.0) && (global_min >= 0)
  
  if (already_normalized && !force_normalize) {
    .log("  [OK] Values already in reflectance scale (0.0 to ~1.0). No normalization applied.")
    if (micasense) {
      .log("  [WARNING] micasense = TRUE was set, but values appear already normalized.")
      .log("  [WARNING] Division by", micasense_factor, "was SKIPPED to prevent data corruption.")
      .log("  [WARNING] To force normalization anyway, set force_normalize = TRUE.")
    }
    norm_reason <- "Skipped -- values already in reflectance scale"
    
  } else if (micasense || force_normalize) {
    if (force_normalize && already_normalized)
      .log("  [!] force_normalize = TRUE. Overriding safety check.")
    .log("  [!] MicaSense camera declared. Applying division by", micasense_factor, "...")
    r           <- r / micasense_factor
    normalized  <- TRUE
    norm_reason <- paste0("MicaSense declared by user (/ ", micasense_factor, ")")
    
  } else if (auto_detect) {
    if (global_max > 1000 && global_max <= 65535) {
      detected_factor <- dplyr::case_when(
        global_max > 30000 & global_max < 36000  ~ 32768,
        global_max > 32767 & global_max <= 65535 ~ 65535,
        global_max > 1000  & global_max <= 10000 ~ 10000,
        TRUE                                     ~ micasense_factor
      )
      .log(sprintf("  [!] High DN values detected (max = %.1f). Applying division by %d...",
                   global_max, detected_factor))
      r           <- r / detected_factor
      normalized  <- TRUE
      norm_reason <- sprintf("Auto-detected (max = %.1f, / %d)", global_max, detected_factor)
    } else {
      .log("  [OK] No normalization needed based on auto-detection.")
      norm_reason <- "Skipped -- no high DN values detected"
    }
  } else {
    .log("  [OK] Normalization skipped (micasense = FALSE, auto_detect = FALSE).")
    norm_reason <- "Skipped -- normalization disabled by user"
  }
  
  .log("  [4/5] Clipping raster with polygon boundary...")
  shp_vect  <- terra::vect(shp)
  r_clipped <- terra::mask(terra::crop(r, shp_vect), shp_vect)
  
  if (!is.null(output_path)) {
    .log("  [5/5] Saving output raster to:", output_path)
    terra::writeRaster(r_clipped, output_path, overwrite = TRUE, datatype = "FLT4S")
  } else {
    .log("  [5/5] Output returned as in-memory SpatRaster (output_path not provided).")
  }
  
  new_range   <- terra::minmax(r_clipped)
  clipped_min <- min(new_range[1, ], na.rm = TRUE)
  clipped_max <- max(new_range[2, ], na.rm = TRUE)
  
  .sep()
  .log("  PROCESSING REPORT")
  .sep()
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %s", "Input raster:",   basename(raster_path)))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %s", "Input polygon:",  basename(if (is.character(polygon_path)) polygon_path else "sf object")))
  .log("")
  .log("  -- Pixel Resolution ----------------------------------------")
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %.4f x %.4f m", "Original resolution (X x Y):", res_orig[1], res_orig[2]))
  if (reproj_raster)
    .log(sprintf("  %-32s %.4f x %.4f m", "Resolution after reprojection:", res_new[1], res_new[2]))
  .log("")
  .log("  -- Coordinate Reference Systems ----------------------------")
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s EPSG:%-6s (%s)", "Raster CRS (original):",
               epsg_raster_orig, terra::crs(terra::rast(raster_path), describe = TRUE)$name))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s EPSG:%-6s", "Raster CRS (final):", epsg_raster_new))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s EPSG:%-6s (%s)", "Polygon CRS (original):",
               epsg_polygon_orig, crs_polygon_orig$Name))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s EPSG:%-6s", "Polygon CRS (final):", sf::st_crs(shp)$epsg))
  .log("")
  .log("  -- Bands & Pixel Values ------------------------------------")
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %d  (%s)", "Number of bands:", nbands, paste(band_names, collapse = ", ")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %s", "Data type:", paste(unique(dtype), collapse = ", ")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %.4f", "Original minimum value:", global_min))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %.4f", "Original maximum value:", global_max))
  .log("")
  .log("  -- Normalization -------------------------------------------")
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %s", "MicaSense camera:",
               ifelse(micasense, "YES (declared by user)", "NOT declared")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %s", "Already normalized (detected):",
               ifelse(already_normalized, "YES", "NO")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %s", "Force normalize:", ifelse(force_normalize, "YES", "NO")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %s", "Normalization applied:", ifelse(normalized, "YES", "NO")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %s", "Reason:", norm_reason))
  .log("")
  .log("  -- Clipped Output ------------------------------------------")
  .log(sprintf("  %-32s %.4f  to  %.4f", "Value range (clipped):", clipped_min, clipped_max))
  if (normalized && clipped_max > 1.0) {
    .log("  NOTE: Values above 1.0 are valid -- highly reflective surfaces")
    .log("  (white tarps, specular water) can exceed the panel reference.")
  }
  .sep()
  .log("  [DONE] Processing complete.\n")
  
  return(invisible(r_clipped))
}
```

### Usage {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-important}
**Run in the console.** This step reads large raster files from disk.
:::

```{r}
#| label: run-prepare-raster
#| eval: true

result <- prepare_raster_clip(
  raster_path      = "C:/Users/Thiago/Desktop/soil_classification/mica/orto_06_21_24.tif",
  polygon_path     = "C:/Users/Thiago/Desktop/soil_classification/contour_IH_geojson.geojson",
  target_crs       = 32617,
  micasense        = TRUE,
  auto_detect      = TRUE,
  micasense_factor = 32768,
  force_normalize  = FALSE,
  output_path      = "C:/Users/Thiago/Desktop/soil_classification/mica/06_21_24_clipped.tif",
  verbose          = TRUE
)

# Visualize clipped result
polygon <- read_sf("C:/Users/Thiago/Desktop/soil_classification/contour_IH_geojson.geojson")
fieldView(result, polygon)
```

---

## Create Training Samples {#sec-samples}

After clipping the image, random training samples must be digitized manually.
Polygons are drawn interactively over representative areas of each class, and
random points are generated inside each polygon.

**Important considerations:**

- Shadows present distinct reflectance values from soil. They can be treated as 
a separate class or merged with the soil class depending on the study objectives.
- Polygon size matters: small polygons may not capture the full spectral variability 
of a class. For soils with visually distinct colors (sand vs. clay), samples should 
cover all variations.
- The display mode can be set to `"NDVI"`, `"GNDVI"`, or `"NDRE"` instead of `"RGB"` 
to enhance contrast between soil and plants before digitizing.

### Function Definition

```{r}
#| label: fn-training-samples
#| eval: true

generate_training_samples <- function(
    mosaic,
    class_names,
    n_samples    = 400,
    display      = "RGB",
    bands        = list(red = 1, green = 2, blue = 3,
                        nir = NULL, rededge = NULL, swir = NULL),
    output_path  = NULL,
    verbose      = TRUE,
    dsample      = 5
) {
  
  .log <- function(...) if (verbose) message(...)
  .sep <- function() if (verbose) message(strrep("-", 62))
  
  if (!is.character(class_names) || length(class_names) < 2)
    stop("  [ERROR] 'class_names' must be a character vector with at least 2 classes.")
  
  display <- toupper(display)
  valid_displays <- c("RGB", "NDVI", "GNDVI", "NDRE")
  if (!display %in% valid_displays)
    stop(sprintf("  [ERROR] 'display' must be one of: %s",
                 paste(valid_displays, collapse = ", ")))
  
  if (display %in% c("NDVI", "GNDVI", "NDRE") && is.null(bands$nir))
    stop(sprintf("  [ERROR] display = '%s' requires bands$nir to be set.", display))
  if (display == "NDRE" && is.null(bands$rededge))
    stop("  [ERROR] display = 'NDRE' requires bands$rededge to be set.")
  if (display == "RGB" && (is.null(bands$red) || is.null(bands$green) || is.null(bands$blue)))
    stop("  [ERROR] display = 'RGB' requires bands$red, bands$green, and bands$blue.")
  
  if (length(n_samples) == 1) {
    n_samples <- setNames(rep(n_samples, length(class_names)), class_names)
  } else {
    if (!all(class_names %in% names(n_samples)))
      stop("  [ERROR] When 'n_samples' is a vector, all class names must be named.")
  }
  
  n_classes <- length(class_names)
  .get_band <- function(idx) terra::subset(mosaic, idx)
  
  display_mosaic <- switch(display,
    "RGB"   = terra::subset(mosaic, c(bands$red, bands$green, bands$blue)),
    "NDVI"  = { idx <- (.get_band(bands$nir) - .get_band(bands$red))   / (.get_band(bands$nir) + .get_band(bands$red));   names(idx) <- "NDVI";  idx },
    "GNDVI" = { idx <- (.get_band(bands$nir) - .get_band(bands$green)) / (.get_band(bands$nir) + .get_band(bands$green)); names(idx) <- "GNDVI"; idx },
    "NDRE"  = { idx <- (.get_band(bands$nir) - .get_band(bands$rededge)) / (.get_band(bands$nir) + .get_band(bands$rededge)); names(idx) <- "NDRE"; idx }
  )
  
  .sep()
  .log("  GENERATE_TRAINING_SAMPLES")
  .sep()
  .log(sprintf("  %-22s %d", "Number of classes:", n_classes))
  .log(sprintf("  %-22s %s", "Classes:", paste(class_names, collapse = ", ")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-22s %s", "Samples per class:",
               paste(paste0(class_names, "=", n_samples[class_names]), collapse = ", ")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-22s %s", "Display mode:", display))
  .sep()
  
  sample_list <- vector("list", length = n_classes)
  
  for (i in seq_along(class_names)) {
    cls  <- class_names[i]
    npts <- n_samples[[cls]]
    .log(sprintf("\n  [%d/%d] Class: '%s'  |  Target samples: %d  |  Display: %s",
                 i, n_classes, cls, npts, display))
    .log("  >> Draw one or more polygons in the editor window, then close it.")
    
    polygons <- fieldView(mosaic = display_mosaic, editor = TRUE, downsample = dsample)
    
    if (is.null(polygons) || nrow(polygons) == 0)
      stop(sprintf("  [ERROR] No polygons digitized for class '%s'. Aborting.", cls))
    
    pts       <- sf::st_as_sf(sf::st_sample(polygons, npts))
    pts$class <- cls
    sample_list[[i]] <- pts
    .log(sprintf("  [OK] %d points generated for class '%s'.", nrow(pts), cls))
  }
  
  .sep()
  .log("  Combining all classes into a single training dataset...")
  training_samples       <- do.call(rbind, sample_list)
  training_samples$class <- factor(training_samples$class, levels = class_names)
  
  if (!is.null(output_path)) {
    sf::st_write(training_samples, output_path, delete_dsn = TRUE, quiet = TRUE)
    .log(sprintf("  [OK] File saved to: %s", output_path))
  }
  
  .sep()
  .log("  SUMMARY")
  .sep()
  .log(sprintf("  %-22s %d", "Total samples:", nrow(training_samples)))
  for (cls in class_names) {
    n   <- sum(training_samples$class == cls)
    pct <- round(n / nrow(training_samples) * 100, 1)
    .log(sprintf("  %-22s %d  (%.1f%%)", paste0("  ", cls, ":"), n, pct))
  }
  .sep()
  .log("  [DONE] Training samples ready.\n")
  
  return(invisible(training_samples))
}
```

### Usage {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-important}
**Interactive step — run in the console only.**  
`fieldView(editor = TRUE)` opens an interactive window that cannot render 
during HTML/PDF generation.
:::

```{r}
#| label: run-training-samples
#| eval: false
options(warn = -1)
# Step 1: digitize polygons interactively and save to disk
training_samples <- generate_training_samples(
  mosaic      = result,
  class_names = c("soil", "plants"),
  n_samples   = 400,
  display     = "GNDVI",
  bands       = list(red = 3, green = 2, blue = 1, nir = 5, rededge = 4),
  output_path = NULL,
  dsample     = 2
)
options(warn = 0)
# Visualize samples over the original raster
fieldView(result, training_samples)
```

```{r}
#| label: load-training-samples
#| eval: true

# Step 2: load pre-saved samples 
training_samples <- sf::read_sf("C:/Users/Thiago/Desktop/soil_classification/training_samples.gpkg")
glimpse(training_samples)
print(training_samples)
```

---

## Machine Learning Classification {#sec-models}

Three machine learning models are trained and compared:

| Model | Algorithm | Package |
|---|---|---|
| `rf` | Random Forest | `randomForest` |
| `svmRadial` | Support Vector Machine (RBF kernel) | `kernlab` |
| `xgbTree` | Extreme Gradient Boosting | `xgboost` |

All models use **k-fold cross-validation** and **hyperparameter tuning** via 
`caret::train()`. The `train_partition` parameter controls the train/test split 
(default: 70% training, 30% test).

### Function Definition

```{r}
#| label: fn-classification
#| eval: true

run_classification_models <- function(
    raster_data,
    training_data,
    models          = c("rf", "svmRadial", "xgbTree"),
    response_col    = "class",
    tune_length     = 3,
    kfold           = 5,
    train_partition = 0.7,
    min_dist        = 0,
    plot_results    = TRUE,
    output_dir      = NULL,
    verbose         = TRUE
) {
  
  .log <- function(...) if (verbose) message(...)
  .sep <- function() if (verbose) message(strrep("-", 62))
  
  .sep()
  .log("  RUN_CLASSIFICATION_MODELS -- Processing Report")
  .sep()
  
  if (!response_col %in% names(training_data))
    stop(sprintf("  [ERROR] Column '%s' not found in training_data.", response_col))
  if (length(models) == 0)
    stop("  [ERROR] 'models' must contain at least one model name.")
  if (train_partition <= 0 || train_partition >= 1)
    stop("  [ERROR] 'train_partition' must be between 0 and 1 (e.g. 0.7).")
  
  if (!is.null(output_dir) && !dir.exists(output_dir)) {
    dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    .log(sprintf("  [!] Output directory created: %s", output_dir))
  }
  
  names(raster_data) <- make.unique(names(raster_data))
  if (inherits(training_data, "sf"))
    training_data <- as(training_data, "Spatial")
  
  n_classes  <- length(unique(training_data[[response_col]]))
  class_lvls <- sort(unique(training_data[[response_col]]))
  n_total    <- nrow(training_data)
  n_train    <- round(n_total * train_partition)
  n_test     <- n_total - n_train
  n_bands    <- terra::nlyr(raster_data)
  
  .log(sprintf("  %-26s %s",       "Models to train:",   paste(models, collapse = ", ")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-26s %d  (%s)", "Classes:",           n_classes, paste(class_lvls, collapse = ", ")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-26s %d  (train: %d | test: %d)", "Total samples:", n_total, n_train, n_test))
  .log(sprintf("  %-26s %d",       "Raster bands:",      n_bands))
  .log(sprintf("  %-26s %d-fold",  "Cross-validation:",  kfold))
  .log(sprintf("  %-26s %d",       "Tune length:",       tune_length))
  .sep()
  
  results       <- list()
  metrics_list  <- list()
  failed_models <- character(0)
  
  for (i in seq_along(models)) {
    mdl <- models[i]
    .log(sprintf("\n  [%d/%d] Training model: %s ...", i, length(models), mdl))
    
    model_result <- tryCatch({
      suppressWarnings(
      RStoolbox::superClass(
        raster_data,
        trainData      = training_data,
        responseCol    = response_col,
        model          = mdl,
        tuneLength     = tune_length,
        kfold          = kfold,
        trainPartition = train_partition,
        minDist        = min_dist
       )
      )
    }, error = function(e) {
      .log(sprintf("  [ERROR] Model '%s' failed: %s", mdl, conditionMessage(e)))
      NULL
    })
    
    if (is.null(model_result)) { failed_models <- c(failed_models, mdl); next }
    .log(sprintf("  [OK] Model '%s' trained successfully.", mdl))
    
    perf         <- model_result$validation$performance
    perf_overall <- perf$overall
    perf_class   <- perf$byClass
    
    required <- c("Accuracy", "Kappa", "AccuracyLower", "AccuracyUpper")
    missing  <- setdiff(required, names(perf_overall))
    if (length(missing) > 0)
      .log(sprintf("  [WARNING] Missing metrics in $overall for '%s': %s",
                   mdl, paste(missing, collapse = ", ")))
    
    metrics_list[[mdl]] <- data.frame(
      model        = mdl,
      overall_acc  = as.numeric(perf_overall["Accuracy"]),
      kappa        = as.numeric(perf_overall["Kappa"]),
      acc_lower    = as.numeric(perf_overall["AccuracyLower"]),
      acc_upper    = as.numeric(perf_overall["AccuracyUpper"]),
      sensitivity  = as.numeric(perf_class["Sensitivity"]),
      specificity  = as.numeric(perf_class["Specificity"]),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    
    if (plot_results) {
      plot(model_result$map, main = sprintf("Classified Map -- %s", toupper(mdl)))
      print(perf$table)
      print(round(perf_overall, 4))
    }
    
    if (!is.null(output_dir)) {
      out_file <- file.path(output_dir, sprintf("classified_map_%s.tif", mdl))
      terra::writeRaster(model_result$map, out_file, overwrite = TRUE)
      .log(sprintf("  [OK] Map saved: %s", out_file))
    }
    
    results[[mdl]] <- model_result
  }
  
  .sep()
  .log("  ACCURACY COMPARISON")
  .sep()
  
  if (length(metrics_list) > 0) {
    metrics_df           <- do.call(rbind, metrics_list)
    metrics_df           <- metrics_df[order(-metrics_df$overall_acc), ]
    rownames(metrics_df) <- NULL
    
    .log(sprintf("  %-14s  %-10s  %-8s  %-10s  %-10s  %-20s",
                 "Model", "Accuracy", "Kappa", "Sensitiv.", "Specific.", "95% CI"))
    .log(sprintf("  %s", strrep(".", 76)))
    for (j in seq_len(nrow(metrics_df))) {
      row <- metrics_df[j, ]
      .log(sprintf("  %-14s  %-10s  %-8s  %-10s  %-10s  [%.4f -- %.4f]",
                   row$model, sprintf("%.4f", row$overall_acc),
                   sprintf("%.4f", row$kappa),
                   sprintf("%.4f", row$sensitivity),
                   sprintf("%.4f", row$specificity),
                   row$acc_lower, row$acc_upper))
    }
    best_model <- metrics_df$model[1]
    .log(sprintf("\n  Best model (overall accuracy): %s  (%.4f)",
                 best_model, metrics_df$overall_acc[1]))
    results[["metrics"]] <- metrics_df
  } else {
    .log("  [WARNING] No models completed successfully.")
  }
  
  if (length(failed_models) > 0) {
    .sep()
    .log("  [WARNING] The following models failed and were skipped:")
    for (fm in failed_models) .log(sprintf("            -- %s", fm))
  }
  
  n_completed <- length(results) - as.integer("metrics" %in% names(results))
  .sep()
  .log(sprintf("  [DONE] %d/%d models completed successfully.\n", n_completed, length(models)))
  
  return(invisible(results))
}
```

### Usage {.unnumbered}

```{r, warning=FALSE}
#| label: run-models
#| eval: true

options(xgboost.verbosity = 0)

band_result <- run_classification_models(
  raster_data     = result,
  training_data   = training_samples,
  models          = c("rf", "svmRadial", "xgbTree"),
  kfold           = 5,
  train_partition = 0.7,
  plot_results    = FALSE,
  verbose = TRUE
)

# Accuracy comparison table
band_result$metrics

options(xgboost.verbosity = NULL)
```

---

## Model Evaluation {#sec-metrics}

Classification performance is evaluated using the following metrics, computed 
per class via one-vs-rest decomposition from the confusion matrix:

| Metric | Formula | Description |
|---|---|---|
| **Accuracy** | (TP + TN) / total | Overall correct classifications |
| **Kappa** | (Acc − E) / (1 − E) | Chance-corrected agreement |
| **Sensitivity** | TP / (TP + FN) | Producer's accuracy / Recall |
| **Specificity** | TN / (TN + FP) | True negative rate |
| **Precision** | TP / (TP + FP) | User's accuracy |
| **F1 Score** | 2 × (P × R) / (P + R) | Harmonic mean of Precision and Recall |
| **Balanced Accuracy** | (Sens + Spec) / 2 | Robust to class imbalance |

### Function Definition

```{r}
#| label: fn-metrics
#| eval: true

plot_classification_results <- function(
    band_result,
    save_dir,
    dpi     = 300,
    verbose = TRUE
) {
  
  .log <- function(...) if (verbose) message(...)
  .sep <- function() if (verbose) message(strrep("-", 62))
  
  .sep()
  .log("  PLOT_CLASSIFICATION_RESULTS -- Processing Report")
  .sep()
  
  if (!dir.exists(save_dir)) {
    dir.create(save_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    .log(sprintf("  [!] Output directory created: %s", save_dir))
  }
  
  model_keys <- setdiff(names(band_result), "metrics")
  if (length(model_keys) == 0)
    stop("  [ERROR] No model results found in band_result.")
  
  .log(sprintf("  %-24s %s", "Models found:", paste(model_keys, collapse = ", ")))
  .sep()
  .log("  [1/4] Extracting metrics from confusion matrices...")
  
  overall_rows <- list()
  byclass_rows <- list()
  cm_list      <- list()
  
  for (mdl in model_keys) {
    perf <- band_result[[mdl]]$validation$performance
    ov   <- perf$overall
    tbl  <- perf$table
    
    overall_rows[[mdl]] <- data.frame(
      model    = toupper(mdl),
      metric   = c("Accuracy", "Kappa"),
      value    = as.numeric(c(ov["Accuracy"], ov["Kappa"])),
      ci_lower = as.numeric(c(ov["AccuracyLower"], NA)),
      ci_upper = as.numeric(c(ov["AccuracyUpper"], NA)),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    
    classes <- rownames(tbl)
    for (cls in classes) {
      tp      <- tbl[cls, cls]
      fn      <- sum(tbl[cls, ]) - tp
      fp      <- sum(tbl[, cls]) - tp
      tn      <- sum(tbl) - tp - fn - fp
      sens    <- tp / (tp + fn)
      spec    <- tn / (tn + fp)
      prec    <- ifelse((tp + fp) > 0, tp / (tp + fp), NA)
      rec     <- sens
      f1      <- ifelse(!is.na(prec) & (prec + rec) > 0, 2 * prec * rec / (prec + rec), NA)
      bal_acc <- (sens + spec) / 2
      
      met_vals <- c(Sensitivity = sens, Specificity = spec, Precision = prec,
                    F1 = f1, `Balanced Accuracy` = bal_acc)
      
      for (met in names(met_vals)) {
        byclass_rows[[paste(mdl, cls, met)]] <- data.frame(
          model = toupper(mdl), class = cls, metric = met,
          value = as.numeric(met_vals[[met]]), stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      }
    }
    cm_list[[mdl]] <- tbl
    .log(sprintf("  [OK] '%s' -- classes: %s", toupper(mdl), paste(classes, collapse = ", ")))
  }
  
  overall_df           <- do.call(rbind, overall_rows)
  byclass_df           <- do.call(rbind, byclass_rows)
  rownames(overall_df) <- NULL
  rownames(byclass_df) <- NULL
  
  base_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 13),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 11), axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
          legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
          strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
  
  model_colors <- setNames(viridis::viridis(length(model_keys), option = "D", end = 0.85),
                           toupper(model_keys))
  class_colors <- setNames(viridis::viridis(length(unique(byclass_df$class)), option = "turbo", end = 0.85),
                           unique(byclass_df$class))
  
  .log("  [2/4] Plotting confusion matrices...")
  cm_plots <- list()
  for (mdl in model_keys) {
    cm_df         <- as.data.frame(as.table(cm_list[[mdl]]))
    colnames(cm_df) <- c("Predicted", "Reference", "Count")
    totals        <- aggregate(Count ~ Reference, data = cm_df, FUN = sum)
    cm_df         <- merge(cm_df, totals, by = "Reference", suffixes = c("", "_total"))
    cm_df$pct     <- cm_df$Count / cm_df$Count_total * 100
    cm_plots[[mdl]] <- ggplot(cm_df, aes(x = Reference, y = Predicted, fill = Count)) +
      geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
      geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%d\n(%.1f%%)", Count, pct)),
                size = 4, fontface = "bold", color = "white") +
      scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "D", direction = -1, name = "Count") +
      labs(title = sprintf("Confusion Matrix -- %s", toupper(mdl)),
           x = "True Class", y = "Predicted Class") +
      coord_equal() + base_theme + theme(legend.position = "right")
  }
  combined_cm <- wrap_plots(cm_plots, nrow = 1)
  ggsave(file.path(save_dir, "confusion_matrices.png"), combined_cm,
         width = 5.5 * length(model_keys), height = 5.5, dpi = dpi)
  .log("  [OK] Confusion matrices saved.")
  
  .log("  [3/4] Plotting overall metrics...")
  overall_plot <- ggplot(overall_df, aes(x = model, y = value, fill = model)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.65, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_errorbar(data = overall_df[!is.na(overall_df$ci_lower), ],
                  aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper), width = 0.18, linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.4f", value)), vjust = -0.55, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = model_colors) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.12), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
                       labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
    facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y") +
    labs(title = "Overall Model Performance", x = NULL, y = "Value") +
    base_theme + theme(legend.position = "none")
  ggsave(file.path(save_dir, "overall_metrics.png"), overall_plot,
         width = 4 * length(unique(overall_df$metric)), height = 5, dpi = dpi)
  .log("  [OK] Overall metrics plot saved.")
  
  .log("  [4/4] Plotting per-class metrics...")
  byclass_clean        <- byclass_df[!is.na(byclass_df$value), ]
  byclass_clean$metric <- factor(byclass_clean$metric,
                                 levels = c("Sensitivity", "Specificity", "Precision",
                                            "F1", "Balanced Accuracy"))
  byclass_plot <- ggplot(byclass_clean, aes(x = model, y = value, fill = class)) +
    geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), width = 0.7,
             color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", value)), position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
              vjust = -0.55, size = 3, fontface = "bold") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = class_colors, name = "Class") +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.12), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
                       labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
    facet_wrap(~ metric, nrow = 2) +
    labs(title = "Per-Class Model Performance", x = "Model", y = "Value") +
    base_theme + theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  ggsave(file.path(save_dir, "byclass_metrics.png"), byclass_plot,
         width = max(10, 3.5 * ceiling(length(unique(byclass_clean$metric)) / 2)),
         height = 10, dpi = dpi)
  .log("  [OK] Per-class metrics plot saved.")
  
  .sep()
  .log("  SUMMARY")
  .sep()
  .log(sprintf("  %-30s %s", "Models evaluated:", paste(toupper(model_keys), collapse = ", ")))
  .log(sprintf("  %-30s %s", "Classes found:", paste(unique(byclass_df$class), collapse = ", ")))
  acc_rows <- overall_df[overall_df$metric == "Accuracy", ]
  acc_rows <- acc_rows[order(-acc_rows$value), ]
  .log("")
  .log("  -- Overall Accuracy ------------------------------------------")
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(acc_rows)))
    .log(sprintf("  %-14s %.4f  [%.4f -- %.4f]",
                 acc_rows$model[i], acc_rows$value[i],
                 acc_rows$ci_lower[i], acc_rows$ci_upper[i]))
  .sep()
  .log(sprintf("  [DONE] All plots saved to: %s\n", save_dir))
  
  return(invisible(list(
    overall_df = overall_df, byclass_df = byclass_df,
    overall_plot = overall_plot, byclass_plot = byclass_plot,
    confusion_matrix = combined_cm
  )))
}
```

### Usage {.unnumbered}

```{r}
#| label: run-metrics
#| eval: true

metrics_output <- plot_classification_results(
  band_result = band_result,
  save_dir    = "C:/Users/Thiago/Desktop/soil_classification/mica/save_test/TEST",
  dpi         = 600
)

metrics_output$overall_plot
metrics_output$byclass_plot
metrics_output$confusion_matrix
```

---

## Soil Background Removal {#sec-mask}

Using the best classification model, soil pixels are masked out, retaining 
only plant pixels for subsequent vegetation index analysis. This step reduces 
noise in spectral index calculations caused by soil background.

```{r}
#| label: soil-mask
#| eval: true

# Create mask: keep only pixels classified as plants (class == 1)
soilmask <- band_result$rf$map == 1
ortosolo <- mask(result, soilmask, maskvalue = 0)

# Save masked raster
#writeRaster(ortosolo, "results/06_21_removed.tif", overwrite = TRUE)

# Compare original vs masked using synchronized maps
m1 <- mapview(raster::raster(result[[1]]),
              layer.name  = "Original",
              col.regions = gray.colors(100))
              #maxpixels   = 7009735)

m2 <- mapview(raster::raster(ortosolo),
              layer.name = "Soil Removed")
              #maxpixels  = 7009735)

sync(m1, m2)
```

---

## Treatment Plots and Buffer {#sec-plots}

Treatment plots are loaded from a geospatial file and a negative buffer is 
applied to avoid edge effects between adjacent plots. The buffer reduces 
pixel contamination at plot boundaries.

```{r}
#| label: fn-plots-buffer
#| eval: true

plots_buffer <- function(file,
                         based        = ortosolo,
                         crs          = 32617,
                         buffer_value = -1,
                         view         = TRUE) {
  
  plt <- st_read(file)
  plt <- st_transform(x = plt, crs = crs)
  plt <- st_buffer(x = plt, dist = buffer_value)
  
  if (view == TRUE) {
    print(fieldView(based, fieldShape = plt, type = 2, alpha_grid = 0.2))
  } else {
    print(plot(plt))
  }
  
  return(invisible(plt))
}
```

```{r}
#| label: run-plots-buffer
#| eval: true

trts <- plots_buffer(
  file         = "C:/Users/Thiago/Desktop/soil_classification/IH_plots_USDA_NxB_geojson.geojson",
  based        = ortosolo,
  crs          = 32617,
  buffer_value = -0.2,
  view         = TRUE
)
```

---

## Vegetation Index Calculation {#sec-vi}

Vegetation indices are calculated using the `pliman` package, which provides 
over 30 pre-configured indices. Three alternative packages are available 
depending on the application:

| Package | Indices | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| [`pliman`](https://tiagoolivoto.github.io/pliman/) | 30+ | Fastest for large rasters |
| [`RStoolbox`](https://bleutner.github.io/RStoolbox/) | 60+ | Most comprehensive |
| [`FIELDimageR`](https://github.com/OpenDroneMap/FIELDimageR) | ~20 | Integrated with fieldView |

::: {.callout-tip}
**Memory note:** Vegetation index calculations are RAM-intensive. On systems 
with 8 GB RAM, limit the number of indices to a maximum of 10 simultaneously.
:::

```{r}
#| label: run-vi
#| eval: true

vi_calculation <- mosaic_index(
  mosaic = ortosolo,
  r      = 3,
  g      = 2,
  b      = 1,
  re     = 4,
  nir    = 5,
  index  = c("R", "G", "NDVI", "NDRE", "GNDVI",
             "NGRDI", "RVI", "SAVI", "TSAVI", "TVI",
             "CVI", "CIG", "BAI", "OSAVI", "MSR",
             "NLI", "GOSAVI", "RDVI", "TDVI", "ARI",
             "MSAVI", "VIG", "WDRVI")
)

# Visualize one VI
fieldView(mosaic = vi_calculation$NDVI, fieldShape = trts, plotCol = "Ntreat")
```

---

## Extract Statistics per Plot {#sec-extract}

Vegetation index values are extracted for each treatment plot and summarized 
using the median (or any other statistic). The output is a data frame with one 
row per plot, ready for statistical analysis.

```{r}
#| label: run-extract
#| eval: false

stats <- cbind(
  st_drop_geometry(trts),
  exact_extract(vi_calculation, trts, fun = "median")
) %>%
  rename_with(~ gsub("median\\.", "", .x))

# Save to Excel
#writexl::write_xlsx(stats, "results/vegetation_indices.xlsx")

# Preview
head(stats)
```

---

## Session Info {.appendix}

```{r}
#| label: session-info
#| eval: true
#| echo: false

sessionInfo()
```
